steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content: With 25 objects, the median is the middle value. The median position is $\frac{n+1}{2} = \frac{25+1}{2} = 13$, meaning the 13th object (when ordered by height) is the median.

steps.title: Build cumulative frequency to locate the interval
steps.content:
Instead of listing all heights, use the cumulative count:
  • 0–100 cm: cumulative count = 8
  • 101–200 cm: cumulative count = 8 + 6 = 14
  • 201–300 cm: cumulative count = 14 + 5 = 19
The 13th value falls between position 8 and position 14, so it lies in the 101–200 cm interval.

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content:
Check which choice falls in the 101–200 cm range:
  • A) 355 cm → in 301–400 range
  • B) 255 cm → in 201–300 range
  • C) 155 cm → in 101–200 range ✓
  • D) 55 cm → in 0–100 range
That matches choice C — C is correct.
